meaning: vt.反对,使对立,使对抗,抗争vi.反对
examples: I would oppose changing the law.
More people favour a single European currency than oppose it.
This party would bitterly oppose the re-introduction of the death penalty.
Many in the shipping industry oppose the speed limit, saying it would be too costly.
The Liberal Democrats, who oppose it, say they will not block new power stations.
